programmes.show_description: ...Experiment: Jess and Ben explore a non-monogamous lifestyle in the hot tub with Olivia and Gage, where Jess faces a big fear. And Kirsty and Tom consider attending a huge orgy.
Constance is on a mission to get the listing for a celebrity singer's £12.5m home, Guy values a quaint cottage in Henley-on-Thames, and Diana takes on a high-end rental.
The East Anglian Air Ambulance crew battle to save driver Gerry's partially amputated hand. Motorcyclist Jamie has sustained multiple injuries following a collision with a van.
Adrian has injuries to his face and has been bitten on his arm breaking up a fight in the city centre. Andy's had a motorbike accident. And Connor's fallen 12 feet off a roof.
Jessica Fostekew and Joe Lycett enjoy a Canarian caper, as they trike through Lanzarote's lunar landscape, sample volcanic vino and cactus croquettes, and tour the lava tunnels.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: In Stratford-upon-Avon, George Clarke helps Laura and Jonnie to transform their mock-Tudor Edwardian house. And in Essex, Nicki's 1920s cottage needs major changes.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: It's time for the hay harvest - a big job of mowing meadows, drying the grass and baling it up - in order to give the animals food for the winter.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Bulgaria, Colin and Paul give their horseback riding a trial trot. In Spain, have Jo and Ken's business-boosting experiments moved the dial?
...the Sun: With Jasmine's husband Jon out of action after his accident, her dad comes to help decorate. And fellow presenter Laura Hamilton treats Jasmine to a trip into Málaga. (Ep4)
John and Carole have different ideas on where to buy their first home together. Will it be the wild coastline of west Wales or charming Carcassonne in the south of France?
Sharon and Brendon want to buy a holiday home beside beautiful Lake Garda, but they each have very different priorities... Can Lucy Alexander fulfil their dreams?
Dundee. The first host, alarm company owner Bill, plans a belt-busting menu for his guests, including the alarmingly named 'death by peanut butter' dessert. (Ep1/5)
Dundee. Architectural technician Alex wants to give his guests an education in fine dining, with a complex menu and fine wines, and introduces them to his 'fur babies'. (Ep2/5)
Dundee. Cat-loving vegetarian Hannah serves up an Italian menu and has a cunning plan to win over her meat-eating guests. (Ep3/5)
Dundee. Self-styled food critic Phil has been less than impressed with the standard of food so far and has high hopes for his night. But he makes a schoolboy error. (Ep4/5)
Dundee. Kim plans a 'Yorkshire-themed' menu - featuring dishes from Spain and Thailand - accompanied by a Scottish choir singing American songs. (Ep5/5)

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: This episode celebrates one of Kirstie's biggest builds as we look back at how Keeley and Terence completely overhauled their Woodford home beyond recognition.
Laura and Daniel want their son to have the same idyllic childhood that Laura had on Mersea Island in Essex. But converting an old beach house on a tiny budget becomes a nightmare.
Steve and Crispin prepare their sheep for a speed shearing competition on Dartmoor. In Looe, everyone gears up for the big one-mile wild swim from St George's Island back to the town beach.
...for Stand Up To Cancer: Molly-Mae Hague, Richard Herring, JoJo Siwa and Babatunde Aléshé make decorated traybakes and tarte tatin, and recreate childhood toys in biscuits.
Fifteen-year-old Meldred is rushed into A&E after being stabbed in the thigh by a stranger while walking to school. Sports commentator Andrew has a mysterious rash on his ankle.
A security guard manages a violent incident with a patient. Jack, who's 33, is rushed into A&E after falling from a horse. And seven-year-old Lyla has a finger infection.
This time it's Race Across the World, Married at First Sight Australia, The Apprentice, Last One Laughing, Secrets of the Bees, Just One Thing and Celebrity Ex on the Beach.
What do Britain's sharpest armchair critics think of Virgin Island, The Four Seasons, Race Across the World, The Piano, I Kissed a Boy and Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen?
Constance is on a mission to get the listing for a celebrity singer's £12.5m home, Guy values a quaint cottage in Henley-on-Thames, and Diana takes on a high-end rental.

programmes.show_name: Help! I Bought It at Auction...
programmes.show_description: ...with Sarah Beeny: In Nottinghamshire, Laura turns a century-old bungalow with ivy growing under the wallpaper into a dream home. In Stoke-on-Trent, Tariq takes on a former library. (Ep2)
A couple create an ambitious floating home, George and Will make a magical vista in their garden flat, and two siblings build their own mobile homes away from home.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...

Peterborough. Part-time rapper Warren waxes lyrical while he cooks his Spanish cuisine, and treats his guests to a musical interlude. (Ep1/5)
Peterborough. Extremely competitive entrepreneur Donna plans a colourful evening of delicious food and relaxed fun. (Ep2/5)
Peterborough. Glamour model and columnist Susie is out to prove that she's not the 'dizzy blonde' her guests think, and they discover there's more to Susie than meets the eye... (Ep3/5)
Peterborough. Straight-talking businesswoman Vicki, who's been very direct with her rival diners, surprises her guests by reading out a heartfelt poem she's written about them. (Ep4/5)
Peterborough. Veterinary nurse Robin hosts the final night with an animal theme and well-presented cuisine. Is he the dark horse of the competition? (Ep5/5)
In Sheffield, first host Lizzy brings the heat with a Mexican-themed night filled with fiesta vibes, a build-your-own burrito bonanza and an unexpected surprise... (Ep1/5)
In Sheffield, Sally hopes to impress with a Shanghai menu, but one guest's starter leaves them tongue tied. After showcasing her piano skills, can she strike the right chord? (Ep2/5)
In Sheffield, Leon injects some spice with a scorching Caribbean supper. But a fiery main makes a few stomachs rumble. Will his entertainment impress, or will his efforts backfire? (Ep3/5)
In Sheffield, Lindsey serves up some antioxidant-stuffed dishes. But her soup is ruthlessly likened to baby food and her main makes a guest retch at the table. (Ep4/5)
In Sheffield, Ambrose hopes that his tried-and-tested dishes can bag him the grand. After a barrage of questions and some cocky competitive chat, has his night won out? (Ep5/5)
A couple create an ambitious floating home, George and Will make a magical vista in their garden flat, and two siblings build their own mobile homes away from home.
In the West Midlands, Kirstie helps neurosurgeon Naomi escape from a crowded house, while Phil meets couple Mo and Jon, who want completely different things from a home.
Jill and Ronnie are finally taking the plunge and moving to France. Jasmine Harman shows them five superb options in Languedoc-Roussillon for their £250k budget. Are any of them perfect?
Sue and Tony want a holiday bolthole in the French city of Carcassonne. With a property already on their radar, can any of Craig Rowe's options for their £100k win them over?
Gary and Alison are looking for a rural holiday home in the Haute-Vienne area of central France. Can Laura Hamilton find them the perfect place for their budget of £100k?
Sharon and Brendon want to buy a holiday home beside beautiful Lake Garda, but they each have very different priorities... Can Lucy Alexander fulfil their dreams?

Artist Sarah returns to her roots in the Durham Dales to build a cannabis-based home. But costs go through the roof. Can she get her hempcrete hub of a home over the line?
(2002) Action thriller starring Vin Diesel as Xander Cage: a thrill-seeking extreme sports enthusiast, stuntman and rebellious athlete-turned-reluctant spy. With Samuel L Jackson.
